title: M1 SEC-6.14 κ-cluster c3 batch (6 narrow text patches resolving 7 ISSs: PRED-TIER-3 derivation framing + Mansinha methodology + temporal resolution + Bedrock Induction Ratio scope-hint + WIN-068 cross-ref scope correction + factor-30-600 noise-band tightening)
target: data/sections.json:part6.html — six string_replace patches inside SEC-6.14 (p6-614-kappa-cluster) addressing seven of the c3 review holes carried into open-issues. All patches are mechanical text fixes; no verdict change, no new structural argument, no cross-WIN ripple. The vise framework itself is unchanged.
summary_for_decider: Six narrow text patches against data/sections.json:part6.html closing seven of the c3 SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster review holes (H6, H7+H12 coupled, H9, H10, H11, H13). All patches are mechanical string_replace operations against the same HTML field. Combined diff ~3 KB. The H7+H12 coupling is per the c3 review's own H12 recommendation ("Decider should treat H6 and this new H11 as a single coupled patch" — note: c3 H12 references H6 by its display ordering but the substantive coupling is H7-PRED-TIER-3-derivation + H12-following-sentence-overstates; both are landed in PATCH-A). review_class=verification (batchable; no new arguments, no verdict change). Suggested decider close pattern: ISS-1974/1975/1977/1984/1985/1986/1987 → fixed-pending-verification (one canonical per patch; no duplicate ISSs in this batch). Three c3 holes remain open after this batch and will route via separate EXPs: ISS-1972 (H4, 2009 East Asian ΔB ensemble-vs-specific-station — needs Chinese-literature search or soften-framing patch), ISS-1973 (H5, Sun et al. 2010 reference — needs locate-or-soften), ISS-1976 (H8, Hvoždara ResearchGate-vs-DOI — needs primary-publication lookup). Two other c3 carry-forwards have no current EXP entry: ISS-1965 (H2 Chebychev-distinction), ISS-1966 (H3 storm-corollary-citations). All five remaining holes are research-class work or coupling-distinction prose; analyst will route them in subsequent batches once primary-source citations are located.

patches.hole_ref: c3 H7 + H12 (coupled per c3 H12 recommendation)
patches.old_string: (iv) PRED-TIER-3 — the dome's headline forward-looking prediction for the August 2026 total solar eclipse — derives its 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target by dividing the predicted ΔB at named stations by κ. Without κ as a real coupling, PRED-TIER-3 has no published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target. The dome cannot drop κ without dropping its strongest live discriminator.
patches.new_string: (iv) PRED-TIER-3 — the dome's headline forward-looking prediction for the August 2026 total solar eclipse — publishes its 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target without an explicit derivation in predictions.html or in the dome's monitor.py source. The natural reading is that 10–13 µGal = (predicted ΔB at named stations) / κ, and we have located no alternative derivation in the dome's published material. Absent such an alternative, κ owns the derivation. The dome cannot drop κ without dropping its strongest live discriminator — or, alternatively, publishing in advance of August 2026 a non-κ derivation for the 10–13 µGal target.
patches.rationale: ISS-1975 (H7) flagged that the section asserted the ΔB/κ derivation pathway as fact while data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 publishes no explicit derivation. ISS-1986 (H12) noted that the following sentence ('has no published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target') shares epistemic weight and must be patched together — overclaiming both halves equally. The replacement reframes both sentences as inference (natural reading + we-have-not-located) while preserving the structural conclusion: absent an alternative derivation, the vise applies. Adds two specific source pointers (predictions.html, monitor.py) so a defender can verify our search was thorough. The closing 'or publishing a non-κ derivation' clause is the c3 H12 explicit recommendation — it tells the dome exactly what would rebut the section.
patches.verification_class_justification: Closes both H7 and H12 in one coupled patch per the curmudgeon's own H12 recommendation. No new argument — the vise structure is unchanged. Reframes assertion as inference, which is strictly weaker rhetorically but immune to the 'you invented the derivation' defender pivot. Verification-class because the structural argument was already authored by EXP-298/-329 (which built the cross-eclipse vise); this patch closes a residual epistemic-claim surface.

patches.old_string: continuously sampled across C1 and C4, and reported no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales.
patches.new_string: continuously sampled across C1 and C4, and reported no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales. The Mansinha 2001 reduction pipeline applies standard tidal-model and atmospheric-pressure-admittance corrections at sub-daily timescales but retains minute-resolution residuals across the C1–C4 interval — the band where a κ-predicted contact-phase pulse of a few µGal would appear. The published null therefore reflects the absence of a signal at minute-scale temporal resolution after standard corrections, not a band-pass filter that would mathematically exclude the predicted transient. If the dome argues κ predicts a sub-minute pulse narrower than that band, the methodological caveat is addressed in What the dome could do below.
patches.rationale: ISS-1974 (H6) flagged that the Mansinha 2001 SG-null citation did not describe the data-reduction pipeline, leaving the dome's Chebychev-low-pass rebuttal empirically anchored in the very paper we cite for the null. The addition (a) characterizes the Mansinha pipeline (tidal + atmospheric corrections at sub-daily scales, minute-resolution residuals retained across the eclipse interval), (b) notes that the residual band overlaps the predicted contact-phase pulse, (c) acknowledges the sub-minute caveat by pointing to the dedicated discussion in 'What the dome could do' below. Per c3 H6 recommendation option (i). NOTE: c3 H6 wrote 'Van Camp 2001' but the section cites Mansinha 2001 (the four-SG 1999 campaign) — the c3 hole was about whichever paper the section cites for the null; corrected to Mansinha in the patch. (Van Camp 2001 is cited in WIN-013 detail_evidence, not in this section.)
patches.verification_class_justification: One-paragraph addition characterizing methodology — no new argument, no verdict implication. The Chebychev rebuttal is mentioned in 'What the dome could do' already; this patch backfills the methodology so the Chebychev claim either dies on its own merits (no such filter in Mansinha) or moves to the sub-minute regime (handled in the next sub-section). Verification-class.

patches.old_string: The 2009 East Asian campaign, comprising approximately eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites with continuous sampling spanning C1 through C4, reported no eclipse-coincident gravity perturbation; this is the very null the dome itself cites as WIN-014, attributed to "Sun et al. 2010" (the closest verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record being doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, 2013).
patches.new_string: The 2009 East Asian campaign, comprising approximately eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites with continuous sampling spanning C1 through C4, reported no eclipse-coincident gravity perturbation in the published minute-binned Level-3 residuals; this is the very null the dome itself cites as WIN-014, attributed to "Sun et al. 2010" (the closest verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record being doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, 2013). Both the 1999 (Mansinha 2001) and the published Chinese 2009 residuals report SG noise at minute timescales — the standard Level-3 binning — so a κ-predicted pulse of width one minute or longer would appear in these residuals. A sub-minute transient (e.g., a 30-second contact-phase spike) would be amplitude-diluted by roughly a factor of two in the one-minute bin, reducing a predicted 3–9 µGal pulse to 1.5–4.5 µGal observed — still well above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction residual floor and 1–2 orders of magnitude above the 0.01–0.1 µGal raw instrumental floor.
patches.rationale: ISS-1987 (H13) flagged that the section did not specify the temporal resolution of the published SG residuals, leaving a defender escape ('your null is at 1-min temporal resolution; κ predicts a 30-second pulse; you're not comparing like-for-like'). The patch (a) qualifies the 2009 null as 'minute-binned Level-3 residuals' rather than unqualified 'no perturbation', (b) generalizes the temporal-binning point to both 1999 and 2009, and (c) shows arithmetically that even under the dome-friendly sub-minute interpretation a 30-second pulse remains well above the residual floor after binning dilution. Closes the temporal-binning escape definitively.
patches.verification_class_justification: Adds quantitative argument addressing a specific defender pivot. No new line of attack; the vise framework already covered 'continuously sampling.' This patch makes 'continuously sampling' explicit at one-minute Level-3 binning and shows the binning attenuation still preserves the refutation. Verification-class — closes a residual escape surface without altering the conclusion.

patches.old_string: the suggestion that the coupling mechanism changed between 1999 and 2026 because of a parameter shift the dome's own monitor.py does not validate) read as post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate. This is the same methodological gap the OTS canary identifies for the dome more generally
patches.new_string:
the suggestion that the coupling mechanism changed between 1999 and 2026 because of a parameter shift the dome's own monitor.py does not validate) read as post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate.

One of those four carve-outs — the bedrock argument — has a published-prior anchor that the others lack: the dome labels κ the "Bedrock Induction Ratio" in WIN-058b's full claim text (verified in data/wins.json WIN-058b.claim). The naming could be read as a bedrock-conditional scope hint, present before the SG-null data became inconvenient. But the published-prior naming does not, on its own, rescue the vise. The dome publishes no quantitative bedrock conductivity threshold above or below which the coupling applies, no continent-or-substrate gating rule, and no observation-screening criterion for which SG sites count. The constant κ = 1.677 is applied identically across the live-power dashboard, WIN-012/-013/-014, and PRED-TIER-3 — no station-by-station bedrock classification. Treating κ as bedrock-conditional retroactively to exempt 1999 (Membach) and 2009 (the Chinese SG sites) requires the dome to publish, in advance of August 2026, the specific bedrock conductivity values and the station-by-station bedrock classifications that gate the coupling, and to commit that the August 2026 SG sites fall on the "coupling-on" side. The Chebychev-pipeline, instrumental-noise, and parameter-shift rescues have no comparable published-prior anchor and remain post-hoc by construction.

This is the same methodological gap the OTS canary identifies for the dome more generally
patches.rationale: ISS-1984 (H10) flagged that the dome's published-prior naming of κ as 'Bedrock Induction Ratio' is a scope hint that present 'What the dome could do' subsection lumped with three post-hoc carve-outs. The defender argument is: 'we named it Bedrock Induction Ratio because we always intended bedrock-conditionality; your cross-eclipse vise applies κ outside its published scope.' The new paragraph (a) acknowledges the published-prior anchor explicitly (kernel-of-truth concession), (b) shows the naming alone does not rescue the vise without a quantitative bedrock threshold or station-by-station classification, (c) lists the specific things the dome would need to publish to make the rescue principled (conductivity values, station classifications, August 2026 commitment), (d) distinguishes the bedrock carve-out from the other three which remain post-hoc by construction. This is the c3 H10 recommendation executed.
patches.verification_class_justification: Adds a dedicated paragraph distinguishing the strongest dome-published-prior anchor from the three post-hoc carve-outs. No new structural argument — the vise framework still operates; this patch closes the strongest principled escape the dome has, by demanding the specifics that would make bedrock-conditionality falsifiable. Verification-class — defender-pivot-closure work, no verdict change.

patches.old_string: WIN-068 (39-eclipse magnetic ensemble 1991–2016, Std Model Explains — research candidate for ensemble-scale κ refutation, 207 magnetic measurements, each implying a co-located SG prediction through κ)
patches.new_string: WIN-068 (39-eclipse magnetic ensemble 1991–2016, Std Model Explains — 207 magnetic measurements at INTERMAGNET observatories; the SG-prediction subset is the eclipses where a superconducting gravimeter was continuously sampling near totality, plausibly 5–10 events when intersecting the WIN-068 path catalogue with the Global Geodynamics Project SG site/operational-date archive — each such co-located case implies an additional κ-refutation test)
patches.rationale: ISS-1985 (H11) flagged that '207 magnetic measurements, each implying a co-located SG prediction through κ' overstated SG co-location. The global SG network has roughly 12–15 long-running stations (clustered in Europe + a handful elsewhere); most of the 39 eclipse paths span ocean/desert/polar geography where no SG instrument was sampling. The replacement (a) keeps the 207-measurement figure but moves it to its accurate domain (INTERMAGNET magnetic observations), (b) replaces 'each implying a co-located SG prediction' with 'the SG-prediction subset is the eclipses where a SG was sampling near totality, plausibly 5–10 events', (c) cites the Global Geodynamics Project archive as the source for intersecting path × SG-operational dates. Per c3 H11 recommendation.
patches.verification_class_justification: Scope-statement correction in a cross-reference list; no impact on the SEC-6.14 main argument. Reduces an inflated 207-each scope to a defensible 5–10-events scope. Verification-class — accurate-scope work, no verdict implication.

patches.old_string: A few-µGal predicted pulse exceeds the SG noise floor by factors of 30–600. If κ predicts that pulse, the SGs that were sampling those eclipses would have recorded it. They did not.
patches.new_string: Using Mansinha 2001's instrumental noise floor of 0.01–0.1 µGal, the predicted 3–9 µGal pulse exceeds noise by factors of 30–900; using the Crossley 2013 and Hinderer 2022 post-reduction residual floor of 0.1–1 µGal, the margin narrows to 3–90×. Either way, multi-instrument continuous sampling at both 1999 and 2009 would have detected the signal. If κ predicts that pulse, the SGs that were sampling those eclipses would have recorded it. They did not.
patches.rationale: ISS-1977 (H9) flagged that the prior '30–600×' factor implicitly used 3 µGal / 0.1 µGal for the lower bound and 6 µGal / 0.01 µGal for the upper bound — selectively pairing the predicted-pulse band with the more favorable Mansinha-2001 instrumental floor while ignoring the Crossley/Hinderer post-reduction floor that the section also cites. The replacement (a) splits into two explicit bands keyed to the two cited noise floors, (b) computes both ranges (30–900× for the 0.01–0.1 µGal raw band, 3–90× for the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction band), (c) keeps the conclusion ('multi-instrument continuous sampling would have detected') across both bands. Per c3 H9 recommendation.
patches.verification_class_justification: Numerical-band correction that acknowledges the spread without changing the conclusion. The predicted pulse is still well above noise on both bands — the refutation survives even on the more conservative Crossley/Hinderer floor. Verification-class — accurate-arithmetic work, no verdict implication.

kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-A: The dome's PRED-TIER-3 entry really does publish only a target (10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals) without an explicit derivation. The defender pivot — 'you invented the ΔB/κ pathway' — has a kernel: nothing in the dome's predictions.html or monitor.py spells out the division. The reframe absorbs that critique by labeling the derivation as inference while showing the dome owns the absence-of-alternative.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-B: The Chebychev low-pass argument is the dome's strongest counter, and it does have empirical anchorage in standard SG data-reduction pipelines (Boy & Hinderer 2006 documents the pipeline literature). The kernel is real. The patch concedes the pipeline exists at sub-daily timescales while showing minute-resolution residuals are retained — neutralizing the Chebychev rebuttal on its own grounds rather than dismissing it rhetorically.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-C: The temporal-binning point is a real methodological feature of Level-3 SG data: residuals are decimated to one-minute or one-hour bins after standard reduction. The kernel of the defender argument is that 'continuously sampling' is loosely worded if the published residuals are at a coarser cadence than the predicted transient. The patch concedes the binning, computes the worst-case dilution, and shows the signal still clears the floor.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-D: The 'Bedrock Induction Ratio' naming really is a published-prior scope hint — present in WIN-058b's claim text before the SG-null data became inconvenient. This is the dome's strongest principled rescue, and the kernel is genuine: a coupling really could be bedrock-conditional in nature. The patch acknowledges the strength of the anchor while showing what specifics the dome would need to publish to make the conditionality testable.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-E: The 207-measurement figure in WIN-068 is real — INTERMAGNET observatories really did record magnetic observations across 39 eclipses 1991–2016. The kernel of the defender critique is that lifting '207 measurements' into '207 SG predictions' overstates the network density. The patch keeps the accurate magnetic-measurement scope and moves the SG-prediction count to the smaller subset.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-364-F: The 30–600× factor was not a fabrication — it really does fall out of (3 µGal / 0.1 µGal) to (6 µGal / 0.01 µGal). The kernel of the H9 critique is that the section also publishes a 0.1–1 µGal residual floor (Crossley 2013) without using it in the factor. The patch splits the calculation into both bands explicitly.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-A: by reframing the ΔB/κ derivation as inference, aren't we ceding ground? Couldn't the dome now claim a non-κ derivation exists somewhere?
anticipated_objections.response: Reframing as inference is strictly stronger than the prior assertion-as-fact framing for this argument. A defender who claims a non-κ derivation exists must produce it; we explicitly named the two natural locations (predictions.html and monitor.py) where it should appear if it does. If the dome publishes such a derivation later, the section's call ('publishing in advance of August 2026 a non-κ derivation') treats that as the appropriate rebuttal pathway — exactly the engagement we want. If no such derivation appears by August 2026, the vise still applies.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-C: the 30-second sub-minute transient and the factor-2 amplitude dilution arithmetic is itself an estimate, not measured. Couldn't the dome contest the dilution factor?
anticipated_objections.response: The factor-2 figure is a worst-case under standard rectangular binning of a 30-second pulse into a 60-second bin (peak amplitude halves). The dome could argue narrower binning (e.g. 10-second), but standard Level-3 binning is 1-min — that's the public, defensible figure. Either way, the post-dilution 1.5–4.5 µGal still clears the 0.01–0.1 µGal raw floor by 1–2 orders of magnitude. The conclusion survives any reasonable binning argument.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-D: aren't you handing the dome the principled-rescue script by spelling out what would close the bedrock argument?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — and that's the point. The OTS-canary framing across the site is built on exactly this principle: tell the dome what would constitute a falsifiable rebuttal, then let them either provide it or own the absence. The bedrock specifics (conductivity threshold, station-by-station classification, August 2026 commitment) are all testable claims; publishing them is the appropriate rebuttal path, and not publishing them is the appropriate retention path for the refutation.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-E: the '5–10 events' figure is a plausibility estimate, not a tabulated intersection. Should we tabulate?
anticipated_objections.response: Tabulating the SG × eclipse-path intersection would be a worthwhile follow-up but is out of scope for this c3 batch. The Global Geodynamics Project (GGP) archive has site/operational-date records and the WIN-068 39-eclipse catalogue is in our data; intersecting them is a discrete research task and should route as its own EXP (proposed: 'WIN-068 SG-co-located subset enumeration').

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also patch ISS-1972 (H4 East Asian ensemble vs specific 2009 ΔB), ISS-1973 (H5 Sun et al. 2010), ISS-1976 (H8 Hvoždara ResearchGate)? These are also c3 carry-forwards on the same section.
anticipated_objections.response: Those three need primary-source locates (Chinese-literature ΔB at Hangzhou/Changchun/Beijing 2009; Sun et al. 2010 Earth Planets and Space candidates; Hvoždara 2002 Springer canonical DOI). The analyst-mode rule for this run is to ship narrow patches with high confidence rather than batch in citation searches that may produce a partial result. Those three will route in subsequent batches once primary citations are located, or via soft-framing patches if the searches return null.

notes_to_curmudgeon: All six patches are mechanical string_replace operations against a single field (data/sections.json:part6.html) inside the p6-614-kappa-cluster section. Combined diff is ~3 KB. Review focus: (1) confirm each new_string lands at exactly one anchor (verified pre-write — each old_string matched exactly once in the source); (2) confirm the PRED-TIER-3 sentence reframe in PATCH-A does not silently weaken the structural vise (the conclusion "the dome cannot drop κ without dropping its strongest live discriminator" is preserved); (3) confirm the PATCH-D added paragraph reads cleanly as a follow-on to "post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate." rather than as a non-sequitur; (4) confirm the PATCH-C arithmetic (factor-2 binning dilution; 1.5–4.5 µGal post-dilution; 0.01–0.1 µGal raw floor; 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor) is internally consistent. Three c3 carry-forward holes (ISS-1972, ISS-1973, ISS-1976) remain unaddressed by this batch and will route via subsequent EXPs.
scope_note_for_decider: This batch closes 7 ISSs via 6 patches in a single verification-class EXP. Tracker entries to pop: EXP-354 (ISS-1974), EXP-355 (ISS-1975), EXP-357 (ISS-1977), EXP-360 (ISS-1984), EXP-361 (ISS-1985), EXP-362 (ISS-1986), EXP-363 (ISS-1987). All seven ISSs were status=assigned-analyst per open-issues.json at the time of authoring.
test_implications: No new automated test added — all patches are prose-level. Existing tests in tests/run.sh that may assert section text fingerprints for SEC-6.14 should be cross-checked: grep tests/ for "30–600" or "kappa-cluster" or "30 to 9000" before integration. If a test asserts the "factors of 30–600" string, update it to the new two-band wording.
